channel.title: Automation on Scoutman
channel.description: Recent content in Automation on Scoutman

item.title: ⚜️ Preparing Something Special
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
I have been preparing something special.

It has been quietly taking shape for a while now, and it comes from a frustration I have had for years.

Anyone who has spent serious time with Azure Automation knows the story: too much back and forth, too much portal dependency, too much friction between authoring, testing, debugging, and deployment.

We end up stitching together folders, scripts, manual checks, and half-working approaches when what we really want is a proper development experience.

That gap has bothered me for a long time.

I wanted something that treats runbooks like real source code.

Something workspace-first. Something Azure-connected. Something that feels closer to modern software engineering and much less like trial and error in a browser tab.

That gap has bothered me for a long time.

I wanted something that treats runbooks like real source code.

Something workspace-first. Something Azure-connected. Something that feels closer to modern software engineering and much less like trial and error in a browser tab.

item.title: ⚜️ Episode III of III - Setup your SPFx development in WSL2 ... with PnPWsl2 !
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
Third of a third-part journey is out !

The second episode, was around WSL2 (Windows Subsystem for Linux), THE tool you definitely need to have in your toolbox .

In this next one, we will dive into what in what is about to become indispensable to support your WSL2 journey …

item.title: ⚜️ Episode II of III - Revolutionize your tech workflow with a single command
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
This is the second of a third-part journey : the first episode, was around awareness of the crucial baselines to get started to drive technical projects\teams.

In this next one we will dive into what is now become indispensable for many in the tech community: its opening up new possibilities, blurring the lines between different computing environments, and providing a seamless bridge between what was once a very distinct and separate world.

Step into …

item.title: ⚜️ Episode I of III - A Technical Baseline
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
Get your curiosity caps on, we’re about to embark on a third-part journey (the first one, more to follow) through something that will change the path of your technical projects, … or at least, I’m hoping it will (for better ! not worse 🤠).

So it begins …
